title: What to Buy for Amy's Birthday
summary: Sam and Chloe debate what to buy as a birthday gift for their mutual friend Amy who is turning thirty. They weigh up several ideas before landing on the perfect choice.

body.speaker: Sam
body.text: So, Amy's birthday is next Saturday. We should figure out what to get her.

body.speaker: Chloe
body.text: I know! It's a big one — she's turning thirty. We need to get something special.

body.speaker: Sam
body.text: What about jewellery? She always wears nice earrings.

body.speaker: Chloe
body.text: Hmm, jewellery is personal. What if she doesn't like our taste?

body.speaker: Sam
body.text: Good point. What about a spa day? She's been really stressed lately.

body.speaker: Chloe
body.text: Oh, I love that idea. It would be really relaxing for her. But it might be expensive.

body.speaker: Sam
body.text: We could split the cost between us. That would make it more affordable.

body.speaker: Chloe
body.text: That's a great idea. How much do you think it would cost?

body.speaker: Sam
body.text: I looked at one place online — it was around sixty pounds for a half-day package.

body.speaker: Chloe
body.text: So thirty pounds each? I can manage that. Which spa were you looking at?

body.speaker: Sam
body.text: The one on Maple Street. It has really good reviews.

body.speaker: Chloe
body.text: I've heard of that one. My sister went there and loved it.

body.speaker: Sam
body.text: Perfect. Should we book it now so we can give her the voucher on her birthday?

body.speaker: Chloe
body.text: Yes, let's do that. She's going to love it. It's the perfect treat for turning thirty.

body.speaker: Sam
body.text: Agreed. I'll book it tonight and send you the details.

annotations.exampleEn: I need to figure out how to fix this problem.

annotations.meaningVi: một cột mốc quan trọng (ở đây là sinh nhật lớn)
annotations.exampleEn: Turning forty is a big one — we should celebrate properly.

annotations.meaningVi: mang tính cá nhân, phụ thuộc vào sở thích riêng
annotations.exampleEn: Clothes are very personal as a gift — you might get the wrong size or style.

annotations.phrase: What if she doesn't like our taste?
annotations.meaningVi: Nếu cô ấy không thích sở thích của chúng ta thì sao?
annotations.pronunciation: /wɒt ɪf ʃiː ˈdʌznt laɪk aʊə teɪst/
annotations.exampleEn: What if he doesn't like the colour we chose?

annotations.meaningVi: ngày nghỉ dưỡng tại spa (massage, chăm sóc sắc đẹp)
annotations.exampleEn: She treated herself to a spa day after a difficult month.
He's been stressed lately because of exams.
Let's split the cost of the dinner between us.

annotations.meaningVi: có giá cả phải chăng, trong tầm tay
annotations.exampleEn: The ticket prices are very affordable for students.
We booked a half-day package at the cooking school.

annotations.meaningVi: Tôi có thể lo được khoản đó
annotations.exampleEn: Fifty dollars? I can manage that.

annotations.meaningVi: phiếu quà tặng, phiếu giảm giá
annotations.exampleEn: He gave her a restaurant voucher for her birthday.

annotations.meaningVi: món quà / điều đặc biệt dành cho bản thân hoặc người khác
annotations.exampleEn: A massage is a lovely treat after a hard week.

grammarNotes.title: What about / How about + noun or gerund for suggestions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'What about + danh từ/V-ing' và 'How about + danh từ/V-ing' đều dùng để đưa ra gợi ý trong hội thoại. Ví dụ: 'What about jewellery?' / 'What about a spa day?' Cả hai đều tự nhiên và thông dụng.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'What about + noun/gerund' and 'How about + noun/gerund' are both used to make suggestions. Example: 'What about jewellery?' / 'What about a spa day?' They are very common in friendly conversation and usually trigger agreement or a counter-suggestion.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for recent experiences: 'She's been stressed lately'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Hiện tại hoàn thành tiếp diễn (has/have been + V-ing) diễn tả trạng thái đã kéo dài đến hiện tại. 'She's been really stressed lately' — Cô ấy gần đây đã rất căng thẳng (và vẫn còn). Từ 'lately' (gần đây) thường dùng với thì này.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present perfect (has/have been) to describe a state that started in the past and is still relevant now. Example: 'She's been really stressed lately.' The word 'lately' (= recently) is commonly used with the present perfect. Compare: 'She was stressed' (a finished state) vs 'she's been stressed' (ongoing).

translationVi:
Sam: Vậy, sinh nhật của Amy là thứ Bảy tuần tới. Chúng ta nên quyết định tặng cô ấy gì.
Chloe: Tôi biết! Đây là cột mốc quan trọng — cô ấy tròn ba mươi tuổi. Chúng ta cần tặng gì đó thật đặc biệt.
Sam: Thế tặng đồ trang sức thì sao? Cô ấy luôn đeo những chiếc khuyên tai đẹp.
Chloe: Hmm, đồ trang sức rất mang tính cá nhân. Nếu cô ấy không thích gu của chúng ta thì sao?
Sam: Điểm hay đó. Thế còn ngày nghỉ spa thì sao? Gần đây cô ấy đang rất căng thẳng.
Chloe: Ồ, tôi thích ý tưởng đó. Sẽ rất thư giãn cho cô ấy. Nhưng có thể đắt tiền.
Sam: Chúng ta có thể chia đôi chi phí. Như vậy sẽ phải chăng hơn.
Chloe: Đó là ý hay. Bạn nghĩ nó sẽ tốn bao nhiêu?
Sam: Tôi đã xem một chỗ trực tuyến — khoảng sáu mươi bảng cho gói nửa ngày.
Chloe: Vậy mỗi người ba mươi bảng? Tôi có thể lo được. Bạn đang xem spa nào vậy?
Sam: Cái ở phố Maple. Nhận xét rất tốt.
Chloe: Tôi đã nghe về chỗ đó rồi. Chị gái tôi đã đến đó và rất thích.
Sam: Hoàn hảo. Chúng ta có nên đặt ngay bây giờ để có thể tặng cô ấy phiếu quà trong ngày sinh nhật không?
Chloe: Có, hãy làm vậy. Cô ấy sẽ rất thích. Đó là món quà hoàn hảo cho tuổi ba mươi.
Sam: Đồng ý. Tôi sẽ đặt tối nay và gửi cho bạn thông tin chi tiết.

questions.explanation: Sam says 'Amy's birthday is next Saturday.'
questions.hint: Sam mentions the day at the very start.

questions.prompt: What is Sam's first suggestion for a gift?
questions.hint: Sam links it to something Amy already wears.

questions.prompt: Why does Chloe reject the jewellery idea?
options: It is too cheap for a 30th birthday
Jewellery is personal and Amy might not like their taste
Amy already has too much jewellery
It is too difficult to find the right kind
questions.explanation: Chloe says 'jewellery is personal. What if she doesn't like our taste?'
questions.hint: Chloe worries about their judgement matching Amy's preferences.

questions.prompt: Why does Sam suggest a spa day?
options: Because it is near where they live
Because Amy has been stressed lately
questions.explanation: Sam says 'she's been really stressed lately.'
questions.hint: Sam's reasoning relates to Amy's current emotional state.

questions.prompt: What concern does Chloe raise about the spa idea?
options: It might be too far away
There might not be any spaces available

questions.prompt: How do they solve the cost problem?
options: They split the cost between the two of them
They ask other friends to contribute
They choose a cheaper gift instead
They find a discount code online
questions.explanation: Sam suggests 'We could split the cost between us.'
questions.hint: Two people share the total cost.

questions.prompt: How much does the half-day package cost?
questions.explanation: Sam says 'it was around sixty pounds for a half-day package.'
questions.hint: The price is mentioned when Sam describes what he found online.

questions.prompt: How much will each person pay after splitting the cost?
questions.explanation: Chloe asks 'So thirty pounds each?' and confirms she can manage it.

questions.prompt: Why does Chloe trust the spa on Maple Street?
options: Her sister went there and loved it
questions.explanation: Chloe says 'My sister went there and loved it.'
questions.hint: A family member has personal experience of the spa.

questions.prompt: How will they give the gift to Amy on her birthday?
options: They will give her a voucher
They will take her there on the day
They will give her a card with the address
They will tell her to book it herself
questions.explanation: Sam says 'Should we book it so we can give her the voucher on her birthday?'
questions.hint: It is a physical piece of paper that confirms the booking.

Sam: What about jewellery? She always wears nice earrings.
Chloe: Hmm, jewellery is personal. What if she doesn't like our {{b3}}?

Chloe: So thirty pounds each? I can manage that. Which spa were you looking at?

Chloe: I've heard of that one. My sister went there and loved it.

blanks.explanation: 'figure' — 'figure out' means to decide or determine something.
'special' — for a 30th birthday, Chloe says they need something special.
'taste' — 'our taste' means the style or preferences we have when choosing things.
'stressed' — Sam says Amy has been really stressed lately, making a spa day appropriate.
'expensive' — Chloe worries the spa might cost too much.
'split' — 'split the cost' means to divide the total price between two or more people.
'half-day' — Sam found a half-day package for sixty pounds.
'reviews' — customer reviews are opinions left by people who have visited a place.
'book' — to book means to reserve a service or place in advance.
'treat' — a treat is something special and enjoyable, especially for a birthday.
'details' — the booking details include the date, time, and confirmation.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Sam and Chloe choose a spa day because Amy has been stressed. Is it better to give a gift that addresses someone's current needs, or one that reflects their long-term interests and personality? What makes a gift truly thoughtful?
